title: Eva Pronunciation
wikitext:
[]: Eva Pronunciation             Quote Post by Recall » Mon Nov 10, 2008 12:59 pm Ok i've just watched the entire platinum collection of the NGE series, as we all know it rules, i even enjoyed the original pschyological endings. I watched them with the english dub, as i found the subtitles to be incorrect and too vague to watch it with the original Japanese dub. I've just started to watch EOE with the english dub and the most notable change is the pronunciation of the world Eva. In the series, it was said with a capital E (EEva), yet in EOE its said with a e (eh-va). Why the difference? its the same cast, so why would they change its pronunciation from the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 10, 2008 1:02 pm Haha, this reminds me of a younger, more noobish me, when I asked the same thing. The answer I got: "Dub sucks".

[]:             Quote Post by Recall » Mon Nov 10, 2008 1:07 pm Its the answer I was expecting but just hoping there was more to it, oh well. Thanks :)

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Nov 10, 2008 2:06 pm could be that manga which released eoe instead of adv wanted it pronounced that way. ehva also coresponds to the original Japanese pronounciation

[]: Re: Eva Pronunciation             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Nov 10, 2008 5:34 pm Recall wrote: I watched them with the english dub, as i found the subtitles to be incorrect and too vague to watch it with the original Japanese dub.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:01 pm Axell wrote: ehva also coresponds to the original Japanese pronounciation

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Nov 10, 2008 6:38 pm the difference between "ev-va" and "E"-va is debateable but just don't stress the first syllable too much, I mean "EEEE- vangelion" would sound odd **really, so long as you remember that it's pronounced with a hard "G" as in "gate" not "J" as in "Jam" or "Evangelist": "Evan-gel-ion" not "Evan-Jelly-on"

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Nov 10, 2008 10:34 pm In other words, the manga dub sucks arse. Theres your answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 4:51 pm If you want to hear the correct pronounciation, just listen to the Japanese audio track. Duh.

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Sun Nov 16, 2008 5:20 pm So is it Third Children or Third Child? The Japanese say Third Children, but if we were to say or read that in English, it would sound/look pretty stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 5:36 pm The difference is that "child"/"children" are real words. Evangelion is not.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 16, 2008 5:42 pm Count A'ight wrote: The difference is that "child"/"children" are real words. Evangelion is not.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 6:01 pm Evangelion is not a real word in any real language. It's based on Greek, but it is not a Greek word itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Nov 16, 2008 9:54 pm Count A'ight wrote: Evangelion is not a real word in any real language. It's based on Greek, but it is not a Greek word itself.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Nov 16, 2008 10:47 pm Ancient Greek Etymology From εὐάγγελος "the bringing of good news" from εὖ "good" and ἀγγέλλω "I bring a message" Pronunciation * (Classical): IPA: [e͜ʊaŋɡélion] * (Koine): IPA: [ɛwaŋɟˈɛlio̞n] * (Byzantine): IPA: [eβaŋɟˈelion] Noun εὐαγγέλιον (genitive εὐαγγελίου) n, second declension; (euangelion) 1. a reward for good news 2. good news 3. gospel -wikitionary (take with a grain of salt)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 16, 2008 10:50 pm How about the Greek New Testament? 40,400 Google hits for εὐαγγέλιον, BTW.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Mon Nov 17, 2008 1:39 am "euangelos" and "evangelion" are not the same word...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 2:26 am Count A'ight wrote: "euangelos" and "evangelion" are not the same word...

[]:             Quote Post by Silent Lightning » Tue Nov 18, 2008 3:56 pm In that case, rather than bicker about how the Japanese pronounced it, why not go back to the standard way the Greeks, who invented the word, pronounced it? That would give us you-an- Gel -i-on , which is probably going too far...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 18, 2008 4:31 pm This really isn't as hard as people are making it out to be. :p

able but just don't stress the first syllable too much, I mean "EEEE- vangelion" would sound odd **really, so long as you remember that it's pronounced with a hard "G" as in "gate" not "J" as in "Jam" or "Evangelist": "Evan-gel-ion" not "Evan-Jelly-on"
